hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific complementarity in the ECL2/TM5 interface while reducing off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5 by avoiding generic electrostatic interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: high ddG and positive Δmargin indicate strong SSTR2 binding and selectivity. However, structural quality must be improved to ensure consistent success. Focus on refining the binding geometry and selectively disrupting off-target interactions will enhance selectivity and pass rates.

proposed_changes.rationale: Structural failures suggest excessive clashes in the binding pocket, likely due to overly permissive clash tolerances. Tightening the clash threshold will enforce better geometry and reduce false positives.
To improve selectivity, we must disrupt conserved interactions at non-pharmacophore positions (1, 2, 5, 6, 11, 12) that are likely responsible for off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.
